intro: Halimium pringlei, also known as rock rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. This plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Halimium pringlei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or cream, and is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as to soothe sore throats and coughs.
